## Kitchen & Bath Cabinet Remodeling Kennesaw, GA

URL: https://localblog.cabinetiq.com/blog/kitchen-bath-cabinet-remodeling-kennesaw-ga-2026-guide/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw Contact Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw &#8594; Home / Blog / Kitchen & Bath Cabinet Remodeling Kennesaw, GA | 2026 Guide SEO Kitchen & Bath Cabinet Remodeling Kennesaw, GA | 2026 Guide &#10003; Updated today By Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw &#183; Kennesaw, GA &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-05-14 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-14 Last reviewed 2026-05-14 by Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw Quick Facts Call (404) 531-7588 Area Kennesaw, GA + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Table of Contents How Much Does Cabinet Remodeling Cost in Kennesaw, GA in 2026? What Is the Difference Between Cabinet Refacing and Full Replacement? How Long Does Kitchen Cabinet Installation Take? What Credentials Should a Cabinet Remodeler in Georgia Have? Why Does Kennesaw's Climate Affect Cabinet Material Choices? KENNESAW &mdash; May 14, 2026 &mdash; Published by Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw Kitchen and Bath Cabinet Remodeling in Kennesaw, GA: The 2026 Buyer's Guide TL;DR: Kitchen and bath cabinet remodeling in Kennesaw, GA typically runs $4,500 to $35,000 depending on cabinet grade, kitchen size, and whether you reface or fully replace. Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw (a kitchen and bath cabinet remodeling business in Kennesaw, GA) offers semi-custom and custom options with installation timelines of 2 to 6 weeks for most homes near Town Center, Kennesaw Mountain, and the Big Shanty district. Most homeowners assume cabinet remodeling means ripping everything out — but in 2026, the smarter move for many Kennesaw kitchens is cabinet refacing or door-and-drawer replacement, which preserves your existing boxes and cuts cost by 40-60% versus a full tear-out. The conventional advice says "if you want a real upgrade, gut it." Here's what most overlook: cabinet boxes built before 2005 with solid plywood construction are often structurally superior to many big-box replacements, and replacing only the visible surfaces can deliver an identical visual result for roughly half the budget. That's the contrarian truth driving smarter remodels across Cobb County this year. # Key takeaways Cabinet refacing costs $4,500-$12,000; full replacement runs $12,000-$35,000 in Kennesaw. Project timelines average 2-6 weeks for kitchens, 1-2 weeks for bathrooms. Georgia requires a residential contractor license for jobs over $2,500. Humidity in Cobb County demands moisture-resistant cabinet materials. Verify insurance, license, and NKBA membership before signing any contract. How Much Does Cabinet Remodeling Cost in Kennesaw, GA in 2026? Cabinet remodeling cost is the total price to refinish, reface, or replace kitchen and bathroom cabinetry, including labor, materials, hardware, and disposal. In Kennesaw, GA, kitchen cabinet remodeling averages $8,000 to $25,000 in 2026, while bathroom cabinet projects range from $2,500 to $9,500. Costs vary based on three factors: scope (refacing vs. replacement), cabinet grade (stock, semi-custom, or custom), and kitchen footprint. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, carpenter and cabinetmaker wages in the Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Alpharetta metro averaged $26.84/hour as of May 2024 (source: bls.gov ), and labor is typically 30-40% of any remodel total. Industry-average cabinet remodeling costs, metro Atlanta region, 2026 Project Type Low Mid High Kitchen cabinet refacing $4,500 $8,200 $12,000 Kitchen full replacement (stock) $12,000 $18,000 $24,000 Kitchen full replacement (semi-custom) $18,000 $26,000 $35,000 Bathroom vanity replacement $2,500 $5,000 $9,500 Custom cabinetry (per linear foot) $500 $850 $1,200 Source: HomeAdvisor 2025 True Cost Report, regional Atlanta data. What Is the Difference Between Cabinet Refacing and Full Replacement? Cabinet refacing is the process of replacing only the visible doors, drawer fronts, and end panels while keeping the existing cabinet boxes; full replacement removes and replaces everything. Get help now &mdash; call Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw &#9742; (404) 531-7588 Refacing saves 40-60% versus full replacement and finishes in 3-5 days, but requires structurally sound existing boxes. Refacing vs. full replacement: refacing is the better option because it preserves usable cabinet boxes, generates less landfill waste, and finishes in under a week — provided your existing carcasses are plumb, square, and water-damage-free. Full replacement is the tradeoff option because it costs significantly more and disrupts your kitchen for 2-6 weeks, but it lets you reconfigure layout, add soft-close hardware, and upgrade to deeper drawers. Experts at Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw recommend a refacing assessment before any quote — if box interiors show delamination, water staining, or warping greater than 1/8 inch, replacement becomes the more cost-effective long-term choice. How Long Does Kitchen Cabinet Installation Take? Kitchen cabinet installation is the sequence of demolition, prep, delivery, mounting, alignment, and finish work needed to install new or replacement cabinetry. A typical Kennesaw kitchen cabinet installation takes 2 to 6 weeks from demolition to final hardware, depending on scope. A galley kitchen (a narrow kitchen with cabinets on two parallel walls) installs faster — usually 10-14 days — than an L-shape or U-shape with an island, which can run 4-6 weeks. Custom cabinetry adds 6-10 weeks of lead time before installation even begins because each piece is built to order. The cabinet remodeling process Step 1: In-home consultation. A designer measures the space, discusses layout goals, and reviews door styles and finishes. Typically 60-90 minutes. Step 2: Design and quote. CAD drawings and a written quote follow within 5-10 business days. Step 3: Material ordering. Stock cabinets ship in 1-2 weeks; semi-custom in 4-6 weeks; custom in 8-12 weeks. Step 4: Demolition and prep. Old cabinets removed; walls and local services inspected; floors protected. 1-3 days. Step 5: Installation. Boxes hung, leveled, scribed to walls; doors and drawers fitted. 3-10 days. Step 6: Finish and punch list. Hardware, toe kicks, crown molding, and final adjustments. 1-2 days. What Credentials Should a Cabinet Remodeler in Georgia Have? Credentials are the licenses, insurance policies, and trade certifications that prove a remodeling contractor is legally and professionally qualified to perform work in Georgia. Legitimate cabinet remodelers in Georgia must hold a state residential contractor license for projects over $2,500, plus general liability insurance of at least $500,000. Required and recommended credentials Georgia Residential Basic Contractor License — required by O.C.G.A. § 43-41 for residential jobs over $2,500. Verify at the Georgia State Licensing Board for Residential and General Contractors . General liability insurance — minimum $500,000; $1,000,000 preferred. Workers' compensation — required for any contractor with three or more employees under Georgia law. NKBA membership — the National Kitchen and Bath Association (a trade body for kitchen and bath designers) signals continuing education. Verify at nkba.org . Certified Kitchen Designer (CKD) credential — issued by NKBA after seven years of documented experience and an exam. "Homeowners should always verify a contractor's license number directly through the state board, request a current certificate of insurance, and confirm the certificate names the homeowner as an additional insured for the project duration." National Association of Home Builders — nahb.org Why Does Kennesaw's Climate Affect Cabinet Material Choices? Climate-driven cabinet selection is the practice of choosing materials and finishes that resist humidity, temperature swings, and regional moisture patterns. Kennesaw's humid subtropical climate makes moisture-resistant plywood boxes and conversion-varnish finishes the smarter choice over particleboard and lacquer. Kennesaw sits in a humid subtropical zone with annual average relative humidity above 70% and July highs near 89°F, according to NOAA's National Centers for Environmental Information (source: ncei.noaa.gov ). Cabinet boxes made of standard particleboard absorb moisture, swell at the joints, and fail within 8-12 years in unconditioned kitchens. Marine-grade plywood and properly sealed MDF doors hold up significantly better. The U.S. Census Bureau's American Housing Survey reports that 48.4% of owner-occupied U.S. homes are 40 years or older as of the 2023 survey (source: census.gov ). In Kennesaw neighborhoods like Legacy Park, Kennesaw Acres, and homes along Cabinet IQ Of Kennesaw Expert guide to kitchen and bath cabinet remodeling in Kennesaw, GA.
